Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal: The objective is to transform multiple ERP tables into a single comprehensive event log suitable for process mining. The event log must have at least Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource/Actor and optionally other attributes. Each row represents one event ordered chronologically per case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Cases: The prompt explicitly states to use order_id as the case identifier. So cases will be 1001 1002 1003 1004 1005.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Derive Activities and Map Timestamps: Orders Table Order Created event. Timestamp order_date. Resource sales_person_id. Order Lines Table Item Picked events one per line item. Resource picker_id. Delivery Table Order Dispatched and Order Arrived events split per record. Invoice Table Invoice Issued event. Payments Table Payment Received event.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Combine and Enrich Data: List all events chronologically per order_id. Map resource IDs to names using Resources table. For example Order 1001: Order Created Item Picked ITEM-001 Item Picked ITEM-002 Order Dispatched Order Arrived Invoice Issued Payment Received.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Refinement during drafting: For Order 1002 check chronological order. Order Arrived DLV-5003 Jan 8 10:00 before Invoice INV-1002 Jan 8 10:15. Fix the order: after Order Arrived DLV-5002 then Order Arrived DLV-5003 then Invoice Issued INV-1002.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Format: Check columns Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource/Actor Attributes. Check case IDs 1001 to 1005 covered. Check chronological order per case Verified. Check resource mapping Used names plus IDs. Verify timestamps one more time for all cases OK.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Assumptions: Multiple picking events per order kept separate for granularity. Delivery dispatch and arrival split into two activities. Payment resource mapped to payment method/system. Order 1004 lacks downstream data lifecycle ends at picking. Chronological sorting strictly enforced per case.
